# Chapter 3: Calibration and traceability  (~80 pp)

- 3.1 What a calibration is (8)
- 3.2 Primary, secondary, working standards (12)
- 3.3 The traceability chain and NIST (and BIPM) (14)
- 3.4 Calibration vs validation vs verification (10)
- 3.5 Drift, recalibration intervals, the cost of calibration (14)
- 3.6 Cross-domain examples: voltage, mass, length, frequency, temperature (14)
- 3.7 Failure section: when the standard itself was wrong (8)

**Archetype**: interface (the sensor-to-system handoff).
**Project**: trace one measurement in the reader's life back to a national standard.
**Exercises**: 30.
